start.speaker: SYSTEM
start.text:
The safehouse is a ruin of broken permacrete and charred datascreens. Vesper kneels beside a cracked relay terminal, cataloging damage with the precision of someone who counts every cost.

Avyanna approaches, footsteps careful on debris. The mission went clean — mostly. But Vesper's silence feels weighted.

opening.speaker: Vesper
opening.text: That exit vector you chose. [i]Third-floor balcony, not the ground entrance.[/i] Smart.

choices.text: Ground was a killbox. Seemed obvious.

watching_confirm.speaker: Vesper
watching_confirm.text: I watch everyone's positioning. It's what keeps us alive. But you? You weren't just following orders. You were [i]reading the field[/i].

tactical_mind.speaker: Vesper
tactical_mind.text: Obvious to someone who understands sightlines and crossfire. Most people panic when the shooting starts. You [i]calculate[/i].

silence_noted.speaker: Vesper
silence_noted.text:
[i]Vesper's eyes flick up — sharp, assessing.[/i]

Careful. That's good. But you don't have to guard yourself here. Not with me.

assessment.speaker: Vesper
assessment.text: The way you moved when their reinforcements arrived. You didn't freeze. You pivoted, reassessed, took the high ground. That's not luck. That's instinct.

choices.text: I just did what made sense.

skill_check_tactics_success.speaker: Avyanna
skill_check_tactics_success.text: They came from the south corridor — two-man teams, staggered entry. If I'd held the ground floor, I'd be caught between their advance and the primary exit. Balcony gave me elevation, clear retreat, and forced them into a choke point.

skill_check_tactics_failure.speaker: Avyanna
skill_check_tactics_failure.text: I saw them coming from the corridor... it seemed like the balcony was safer? Less exposure, I think.

why_matters.speaker: Vesper
why_matters.text: Because tactical instinct like that? It's rare. And it's [i]wasted[/i] if it's not refined.

modest_deflect.speaker: Vesper
modest_deflect.text: What makes sense to you would get most people killed. You're thinking three steps ahead without realizing it.

impressed.speaker: Vesper
impressed.text:
[i]Vesper stands, brushing dust from her coat. Her expression shifts — not quite a smile, but something close.[/i]

Exactly. Most people see a firefight. You see a [i]system[/i]. Inputs, variables, outcomes.

offer_lead_in.speaker: Vesper
offer_lead_in.text: I've been doing this for fifteen years. Corps, freelance, wet work I don't talk about. I know talent when I see it.

offer.speaker: Vesper
offer.text: I want to train you. Not as a project — as an investment in someone who already belongs here. Tactical doctrine, engagement planning, threat assessment. The skills that turn instinct into certainty.

choices.text: What would you expect from me?

acceptance_direct.speaker: Vesper
acceptance_direct.text:
[i]Vesper nods once — crisp, final.[/i]

Good. We start tomorrow. Bring questions, not excuses.

expectations_question.speaker: Vesper
expectations_question.text: Honesty. Focus. The willingness to admit when you're wrong — and the discipline to fix it. I don't train people who waste my time.

choices.text: I can do that. Let's begin.

acceptance_after_expectations.speaker: Vesper
acceptance_after_expectations.text: Then we have an understanding. Tomorrow, 0600. Don't be late.

lot_to_ask.speaker: Vesper
lot_to_ask.text: It is. But you've already been doing it without knowing. I'm just making it [i]conscious[/i].

acceptance_after_hesitation.speaker: Vesper
acceptance_after_hesitation.text: Smart choice. We'll make you sharper than any blade in the void.

why_me.speaker: Vesper
why_me.text: Because you remind me of someone I used to know. Someone who died because they had instinct but not [i]discipline[/i]. I won't let that happen twice.

choices.text: Then I won't let you down.

acceptance_honor.speaker: Vesper
acceptance_honor.text:
[i]Vesper's hand rests briefly on Clarity's grip — a gesture of trust.[/i]

I know you won't. Tomorrow. 0600.

not_them.speaker: Vesper
not_them.text: No. You're not. And that's exactly why this matters. You get to [i]survive[/i].

need_time.speaker: Vesper
need_time.text: Fair. This isn't a small thing. But don't take [i]too[/i] long — instinct without training gets people killed.

patience.speaker: Vesper
patience.text: Take your time. The offer stands. But remember — in this life, hesitation is measured in bodies.
